title: Neoporteria transiens
taxonomy: Neoporteria transiens is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Chile. This species is placed in the subgenus Neoporteria of the genus Neoporteria. Within the Cactaceae family, it is part of the subfamily Opuntioideae and tribe Eulychiae. The classification of Neoporteria transiens reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the cactus family.

intro: Neoporteria transiens, also known as Copiapoa transiens, is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Neoporteria, characterized by its small to medium size and cylindrical stems covered with fine spines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria transiens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other plants, to support immune function and overall well-being.
